The ruling itself is understandable, but the evidences are not fully self-contained: one hadith is referred back to as 'المتقدم في المسألة 87' and another is quoted only partially with 'وقد سبق بتمامه في المسألة 35'. That means C-SC-3 is not fully met, while the legal point itself is still mostly complete, so PARTIAL is correct."},{"item_index":1,"agrees":true,"alternative":null,"confidence":0.97,"reasoning":"Agree. This unit depends on prior context more strongly than the others. The pronoun in 'يدفن فيه' lacks an explicit antecedent inside the excerpt, so C-SC-2 fails, and the supporting hadiths are only cross-referenced to other masā'il without their text, so C-SC-3 fails as well. DEPENDENT is the right assessment."},{"item_index":2,"agrees":true,"alternative":null,"confidence":0.9,"reasoning":"Agree. The teaching is substantially understandable on its own: the rule is stated and two rationales are fully present. But the first reason relies on a hadith deferred to 'المسألة 99' and not quoted here, so the unit is not fully closed evidentially under C-SC-3. That makes PARTIAL correct."},{"item_index":3,"agrees":true,"alternative":null,"confidence":0.85,"reasoning":"Agree. The practical ruling is mostly clear, and the عثمان hadith is quoted, but the rejected basis is still externalized: 'الحديث الوارد فيه لا يصح' refers to an unquoted hadith, and 'التلقين المعروف اليوم' points to a practice not fully described within the unit. So it is not fully self-contained, yet it is more than merely dependent; PARTIAL is appropriate."}]}
